title: biscutella laevigata subsp. austriaca
intro: Biscutella laevigata subsp. austriaca is a subspecies of the Biscutella laevigata plant, a flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a perennial herb native to Central Europe, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Biscutella laevigata subsp. austriaca can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to relieve congestion and promote overall well-being.
